description: Autonomous MT5 Trading Bot — 10 core stories from bridge to live trading

userStories.title: MT5 HTTP Bridge Client
userStories.description: As the bot, I need a Python client that connects to the HTTP bridge server, retrieves live ticks, and sends order commands so that all downstream modules have a reliable market data and execution interface.
acceptanceCriteria: BridgeClient class in core/bridge/client.py with methods: connect(), get_tick(symbol), get_account(), send_order(symbol, side, volume, sl, tp)
connect() returns True when bridge server at 192.168.64.1:8080 is reachable with heartbeat < 10s
get_tick() returns dict with keys: symbol, bid, ask, spread, time
send_order() posts to /command endpoint and returns order result dict
Heartbeat polling loop with configurable interval (default 5s), raises BridgeDisconnected on timeout
Unit tests using responses mock — no live bridge required to run tests
python -m pytest core/bridge/test_client.py passes
userStories.notes: Bridge server is already implemented at core/bridge/http_server.py. Client must target 192.168.64.1:8080 (UTM host IP). Use requests library. File-based IPC client is at core/bridge/mt5_client.py — new HTTP client is separate.

userStories.title: Paper Trading Mode
userStories.description: As the bot, I need a paper trading engine that simulates order fills, tracks open positions and closed P&L, and honours risk limits without touching real money.
acceptanceCriteria: PaperBroker class in core/execution/paper_broker.py implementing the same interface as a live broker
place_order(symbol, side, volume, sl, tp) fills immediately at current bid/ask with configurable slippage
close_position(ticket) marks position closed and records realised P&L
get_positions() returns list of open positions with unrealised P&L
get_account() returns equity, balance, margin, free_margin
Daily loss limit enforced: raises DailyLossLimitReached when drawdown exceeds config value
State persisted to bridge_data/paper_state.json on every change
python -m pytest core/execution/test_paper_broker.py passes
userStories.notes: Config lives in config.yaml under bot.mode and risk.*. Paper mode is the default (bot.mode: paper). Never switch to live without --confirm-live CLI flag.

userStories.title: Historical Data Fetcher
userStories.description: As the strategy engine, I need OHLCV data fetched from MT5 via the bridge and cached to disk so backtesting and indicator computation do not hammer the bridge on every run.
acceptanceCriteria: HistoricalDataFetcher class in core/data/fetcher.py
fetch(symbol, timeframe, count) returns pandas DataFrame with columns: time, open, high, low, close, volume
fetch() queries /history endpoint on HTTP bridge
Results cached to bridge_data/history/{symbol}_{timeframe}.parquet using pyarrow
Cache is valid for 1 hour for H1 data; re-fetches automatically when stale
fetch_range(symbol, tf, start_dt, end_dt) paginates requests to bridge and merges results
python -m pytest core/data/test_fetcher.py passes with mocked bridge responses
userStories.notes: Timeframe values match MT5 constants: M1=1, M5=5, M15=15, H1=60, H4=240, D1=1440. Primary instruments are EURUSD and GBPUSD at H1. Parquet preferred over CSV for speed.

userStories.title: Trend Following Strategy (EMA Crossover)
userStories.description: As the bot, I need an EMA crossover strategy that generates BUY/SELL signals from OHLCV data so the execution engine has clear entry triggers with stop levels.
acceptanceCriteria: EMACrossoverStrategy class in core/strategy/ema_crossover.py
Constructor accepts fast_period (default 9), slow_period (default 21), atr_period (default 14)
generate_signal(df) returns Signal namedtuple: side ('BUY'|'SELL'|None), entry_price, sl_price, tp_price, confidence
BUY signal when fast EMA crosses above slow EMA on latest completed candle
SELL signal when fast EMA crosses below slow EMA on latest completed candle
SL placed at entry ± 1.5 * ATR(14); TP at entry ± 3.0 * ATR(14) (2:1 R:R)
No signal generated if spread > 2.5 * average spread (filter noise)
python -m pytest core/strategy/test_ema_crossover.py passes with synthetic OHLCV data
userStories.notes: All indicator math must use pandas/numpy only — no TA-Lib dependency. ATR formula: Wilder smoothing (EMA with alpha=1/n). Knowledge base at research/knowledge-base.md has EMA and ATR formulas.

userStories.title: Risk Manager
userStories.description: As the bot, I need a risk manager that calculates position size from ATR and equity, enforces the 1% per-trade rule, and halts trading when daily loss or drawdown thresholds are breached.
acceptanceCriteria: RiskManager class in core/risk/manager.py
calculate_size(symbol, entry, sl, equity) returns lot size rounded to 0.01, capped at 1% equity risk
Kelly fraction applied: size *= config.risk.kelly_fraction (default 0.25)
check_daily_loss(account) returns True if daily P&L <= -config.risk.daily_loss_limit * equity
check_drawdown(account) returns 'warn' | 'reduce' | 'halt' | 'ok' per thresholds in config.yaml
get_position_adjustment(drawdown_pct) returns multiplier: 1.0 | 0.7 | 0.5 | 0.0
All thresholds read from config.yaml (never hardcoded)
python -m pytest core/risk/test_manager.py passes
userStories.notes: Config thresholds: trailing_dd_warn=0.10, trailing_dd_reduce=0.15, trailing_dd_halt=0.20. Pip value for EURUSD: 10 USD per standard lot per pip. Use 0.0001 as pip size for 5-digit quotes.

userStories.title: Performance Tracker
userStories.description: As the bot operator, I need a performance tracker that computes Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, and win rate from closed trades so I can evaluate strategy quality.
acceptanceCriteria: PerformanceTracker class in core/performance/tracker.py
record_trade(trade_dict) appends to trades list and updates running stats
sharpe_ratio() returns annualised Sharpe using daily returns; returns None if < 30 trades
max_drawdown() returns peak-to-trough drawdown as a positive decimal (0.05 = 5%)
win_rate() returns fraction of profitable closed trades
profit_factor() returns gross profit / gross loss
to_dict() returns all metrics as JSON-serialisable dict
save(path) persists state to JSON; load(path) restores from JSON
python -m pytest core/performance/test_tracker.py passes
userStories.notes: Sharpe target from config: autoresearch.target_sharpe = 1.5. Max drawdown guard: autoresearch.max_drawdown_guard = 0.05. Win rate guard: autoresearch.min_win_rate_guard = 0.45. Use 252 trading days for annualisation.

userStories.title: Autoresearch Loop (8-Phase Engine)
userStories.description: As the bot, I need an autonomous research loop that iterates strategy parameters, runs backtests, checks guard conditions, and persists the best parameter set so strategy quality improves without human input.
acceptanceCriteria: AutoresearchEngine class in autoresearch/engine.py
8 phases: INIT, DATA_FETCH, PARAM_SAMPLE, BACKTEST, GUARD_CHECK, RECORD, SELECT_BEST, PERSIST
run(iterations=50) executes the full loop for n iterations
PARAM_SAMPLE uses Optuna TPE sampler over: fast_period [5,20], slow_period [15,50], atr_mult [1.0,3.0]
GUARD_CHECK rejects any parameter set where Sharpe < 1.5 OR max_dd > 5% OR win_rate < 45%
Best parameters saved to autoresearch/best_params.json on every improvement
Progress logged to autoresearch/research_log.jsonl (one JSON line per iteration)
python -m pytest autoresearch/test_engine.py passes with mock backtest
userStories.notes: Backtest command from config: autoresearch.metric_command. Guard command: autoresearch.guard_command. Use subprocess to invoke backtest engine if running CLI variant. Optuna must be installed (already in requirements.txt).

userStories.title: Knowledge Base Integration
userStories.description: As the autoresearch engine, I need to query the trading knowledge base for strategy rules, risk formulas, and indicator parameters so research iterations are anchored in domain expertise.
acceptanceCriteria: KnowledgeBase class in knowledge/kb.py
load() reads research/knowledge-base.md and indexes sections by heading
query(topic) returns relevant section text for topics: 'ema', 'atr', 'risk', 'sharpe', 'position_sizing', 'drawdown'
get_strategy_params(strategy_name) returns dict of documented default parameters
get_risk_rules() returns structured dict of risk thresholds from KB
KnowledgeBase used by AutoresearchEngine to seed initial parameter bounds
python -m pytest knowledge/test_kb.py passes
userStories.notes: Knowledge base file is at research/knowledge-base.md (written by Agent 1). Contains EMA crossover params, ATR formulas, Kelly criterion, Sharpe calculation. Parse markdown headings (##, ###) as section keys.

userStories.title: Checkpoint and Recovery System
userStories.description: As the bot, I need to pickle full bot state (positions, P&L, best params, research log cursor) to disk so the bot can resume exactly where it left off after a crash or restart.
acceptanceCriteria: CheckpointManager class in core/checkpoint/manager.py
save(state_dict, label='auto') pickles state to checkpoints/{timestamp}_{label}.pkl
load_latest() returns the most recent checkpoint dict or None if no checkpoints exist
rotate(keep=10) deletes oldest checkpoints beyond the keep limit
BotState dataclass in core/checkpoint/state.py with fields: positions, equity, daily_pnl, best_params, research_iteration, mode
Auto-checkpoint triggered every 5 minutes by a background thread
On startup, bot detects existing checkpoint and prompts user (or auto-loads in non-interactive mode)
python -m pytest core/checkpoint/test_manager.py passes
userStories.notes: Checkpoint dir is at /Users/ltmas/trading-bot-workspace/checkpoints/. Use pickle for full fidelity; also write a JSON summary (non-sensitive fields only) alongside each pickle for quick inspection.

userStories.title: Live Trading Mode
userStories.description: As the bot operator, I need a live trading mode that routes orders through the real MT5 bridge (not paper broker), enforces pre-flight checks, and provides a kill switch so real capital is protected.
acceptanceCriteria: LiveBroker class in core/execution/live_broker.py implementing same interface as PaperBroker
Live mode requires --confirm-live CLI flag AND bot.mode=live in config.yaml (dual interlock)
Pre-flight checklist: bridge connected, account equity > 1000 USD, no open positions from previous crash, daily loss not already breached
pre_flight_check() returns (ok: bool, failures: list[str])
All live orders logged to logs/live_orders.jsonl with timestamp, symbol, side, volume, price, sl, tp, ticket
Emergency stop: kill_switch() closes all open positions via bridge and sets mode to paper
python -m pytest core/execution/test_live_broker.py passes with mock bridge
userStories.notes: NEVER remove the --confirm-live interlock. Live broker re-uses BridgeClient from US-001. Config guard: if config.bot.mode != 'live' and --confirm-live not passed, raise LiveModeNotEnabled. The kill_switch must work even when bridge latency is high.

userStories.title: Trend-Following SL/TP geometry fix (v1.1)
userStories.description: As the trend_following strategy, I need wider structural stops and a more attainable take-profit so that the 0%-win-rate problem on real M15 data (caught by the 2026-04-29 backtest) is resolved.
acceptanceCriteria: Default sl_atr_buffer raised from 0.25 to 1.0 in core/strategy/trend_following.py
Default tp_r_multiple lowered from 2.0 to 1.5
autoresearch/params.trend.yaml updated with the new defaults and a comment explaining the empirical rationale
Backtest on 3683-bar EURUSD M15 cache passes the autoresearch guard (drawdown <= 5%, win_rate >= 30%, sharpe > 0)
Existing tests in tests/strategy/test_trend_following.py still pass (defaults updated where they assume the old values)
Lock-canary fixture for params.yaml unchanged; trend_following ships dormant as before
userStories.notes: Source: parameter sensitivity sweep on 2026-04-29 (sl_buf=1.0, tp=1.5 -> GUARD PASS drawdown=2.19%, win_rate=50%, 20 trades vs sl_buf=0.25, tp=2.0 -> 0% win rate). The compendium prescribes 1:2 R:R minimum but real-market geometry on this 38-day window argues 1:1.5 is the achievable target. Document deviation in the strategy docstring.

userStories.title: Pin-bar candle trigger filter (Naked Forex Ch 8)
userStories.description: As the trend_following premium mode, I need a pin-bar / kangaroo-tail candle confirmation at the BoS bar so the FX GOAT compendium's 'specific candlestick triggers' requirement is satisfied and false-signal entries during chop are filtered out.
acceptanceCriteria: core/strategy/candles.py implements is_pin_bar(bar, direction) with the canonical Naked Forex definition: tail length >= 2x body, body in upper third (bullish) or lower third (bearish) of bar range, close beyond the prior bar's close
TrendFollowing premium mode requires is_pin_bar(latest_bar, direction) to return True before emitting a BUY/SELL; HOLD reason='no_pin_bar_confirmation' otherwise
Standard mode is unaffected (no candle gate)
tests/strategy/test_candles.py covers: bullish pin (bottom tail), bearish pin (top tail), Doji rejection, marubozu rejection, body-position rejection, prior-close-direction filter
tests/strategy/test_trend_following.py extended with one premium-mode test that asserts the pin-bar gate fires HOLD when the trigger bar is a marubozu

Backtest on 3683-bar EURUSD M15 with mode=premium runs cleanly (no exceptions). The 38-day window may produce 0 premium trades because the Fib zone + pin-bar AND-gate is intentionally restrictive; the marubozu unit test proves the gate fires correctly when triggered. Standard mode regression check (15 trades, win_rate >= 50%, drawdown < 5%) continues to pass.
userStories.notes: Naked Forex 'Kangaroo Tail' = pin bar. Two-bar prior-close requirement keeps this from triggering on every wick. Don't conflate with Big Shadow (engulfing) — that is a separate Ch 6 pattern, future story. tail_ratio_min lowered from canonical 2.0 to 1.5 at the strategy call site so the Fib + pin AND-gate is not impossibly restrictive on small windows; the canonical 2.0 remains the function default in candles.py.

userStories.title: Updated lock-canary snapshot for trend_following changes
userStories.description: As the OOS-window safety net, the SHA-256 fixture in tests/fixtures/oos_locks_snapshot.json must NOT include trend_following.py — that file is the active development surface. The fixture only locks the OOS-window critical paths.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/fixtures/oos_locks_snapshot.json continues to lock only autoresearch/params.yaml, core/strategy/ema_crossover.py, core/strategy/mean_reversion.py
tests/test_oos_locks.py runs green after US-011 + US-012 changes
config.yaml: autoresearch.enabled remains false
userStories.notes: Sanity check, not a code change. If a future Ralph cycle touches the locked files by accident, this story's tests fail loud.

userStories.title: Operator doc — candle-pattern reading guide
userStories.description: As a beginner operator, I need a concrete walkthrough of the candlestick patterns the bot uses (or considers) so I can read price action on the M15 chart and reconcile what I see with what the strategy emits. Closes the partial-coverage item from the 2026-04-30 compendium audit (#14 'clarify how to read candlestick signals').
acceptanceCriteria: docs/trading/candle-patterns.md exists with YAML frontmatter (title, last_updated, source: 'FX GOAT Mastery Compendium')
Required H2 sections: Anatomy of a Candle, Doji, Marubozu, Hammer / Hanging Man, Engulfing (Big Shadow), Pin Bar (Kangaroo Tail), How the bot uses these signals
Each section has at least one paragraph (>=60 chars body) — no stub headings
Pin-bar section explicitly references core/strategy/candles.py:is_pin_bar and the tail_ratio_min=1.5 strategy-side relaxation
Big Shadow / engulfing section names it as a future-roadmap pattern (not yet in code)
Heading-parser test passes against the new doc
userStories.notes: Naked Forex Ch 6 (Big Shadow) + Ch 8 (Kangaroo Tails) are the canonical references; the FX GOAT compendium §3 names 'specific candlestick triggers' as a Premium-execution requirement. This doc closes the gap between what compendium readers expect and what the bot's mechanical filter implements.

userStories.title: Operator doc — trend-reversal practice exercise
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the FX GOAT compendium's '20 historical instances' trend-reversal training exercise packaged as a repeatable drill. Closes the partial-coverage item from the 2026-04-30 compendium audit (#31 'detail a practice exercise for identifying trend reversals').
acceptanceCriteria: docs/trading/reversal-practice-exercise.md exists with YAML frontmatter
Required H2 sections: Why this exercise matters, How to source 20 historical instances, The drill (per-instance steps), Bot-side reference (reversal_lookback short-circuit), Reflection template
Each section has at least one paragraph (>=60 chars body)
Drill section produces a numbered procedure: pick chart → mark prior swings → annotate reversal → log outcome
Bot-side reference section explicitly names core/strategy/trend_following.py reversal short-circuit and links to the strategy docstring
Heading-parser test passes against the new doc
userStories.notes: Compendium §3 'Trend Reversal Practice Exercise' prescribes the 20-instance drill as part of the Phase-1 Education milestones. This doc closes the loop between the strategy's reversal-detection code and the operator skill of recognising them on a chart.

userStories.title: Operator doc — backtesting how-to
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a copy-pasteable walkthrough of the backtest engine CLI so I can validate strategy changes (and answer FX GOAT compendium §6 Week-3 'integrate Lesson 5 / re-backtest') without grepping the engine source. Closes the partial-coverage item from the 2026-04-30 compendium audit (#32 'describe how to backtest a simple strategy').
acceptanceCriteria: docs/trading/backtesting-how-to.md exists with YAML frontmatter

Required H2 sections: Three goals of backtesting, Where the data lives, Engine CLI essentials, Worked example — locked mean_reversion, Worked example — trend_following standard, Worked example — trend_following premium, Reading guard output, Cross-validation flag, Synthetic-data refusal
Each section has at least one paragraph (>=60 chars body)
Three CLI examples include the literal '--params autoresearch/params.trend.yaml' invocation form for trend_following
Three goals of backtesting section cites Naked Forex Ch 3 (validate edge / build conviction / refine rules)
Synthetic-data section explains the --allow-synthetic safeguard and why it's off by default
Heading-parser test passes against the new doc
userStories.notes: Engine entry point: bot/backtest/engine.py. Existing flags worth surfacing: --params, --symbol, --timeframe, --bars, --metric, --guard, --cv kfold:N, --embargo, --allow-synthetic, --min-trades. The doc must be reproducible from the bot's M15 cache without needing the bridge.
